chunks.header: Digital, Culture, Media and Sport Committee
chunks.text: House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA Tel 020 7219 6188 website www. parliament. uk/cms RtHon Nadine Dorries MP Secretary of State Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport By email 22 September 2021 Dear Secretary of State, Inflicting harm online My Committee heard, last Tuesday, from influencers about influencer culture and the attacks they receive from both anonymous and identifiable users through online platforms. We were shocked to hear the language that is used by those who may otherwise be respectable family people. It wasevenmore shocking to hear that death threats, even from minors, are what influencers expect to see in the conduct of their profession. We also discussed the fact that people send videos designed to trigger epileptic fitsthathave the potential to be fatal. This kind of online harm is, in our view, completely unacceptable. Professional people should not be subject to this kind of mental, and potentially physical, harm. We are particularly concerned about the triggering of epileptic siezures and should notneedtowait for the passage of the Online Safety Bill to address this issue. We ask you to commit to talking, immediately, tothemainplat for ms about finding a technical way to ensure that those with epilepsy donotrisk their health when simply opening a message on an online platform. Yours sincerely Julian Knight MP Chair, DCMS Committee
